Started about a week ago. Nobody wants to go near it after sundown.
The sound comes and goes. Always at the same hour.
Whatever it is, it's getting more frequent.
First it was just the lights. Then the ground started trembling.
We thought it was thunder at first. But the sky was clear every time.
Three nights running, the well water turned brackish. By morning it's fine again.
The old stones started humming. You can feel it through your boots if you stand close enough.
Everyone who's gone to investigate comes back quiet. Not hurt, just... reluctant to talk about it.
It always happens between second and third watch. Like something knows when the guard changes.
The crops in that field wilted overnight. Healthy one day, black the next. No frost, no blight.
People keep finding their doors open in the morning. Locked the night before, wide open at dawn.
The fire in the smithy went cold twice last week. Green flame for a moment, then nothing.
A crack opened in the foundation of the old hall. Nobody heard it happen.
The birds stopped singing over there three days ago. Just silence where there used to be noise.
Fog rolls in from the east now. Used to come from the river. Small thing, but it's wrong.
We set up lanterns along the south path. Every morning, they're snuffed out. Wicks aren't burned down, just out.
The children started drawing the same shape. None of them could say where they'd seen it.
Livestock won't drink from the lower stream anymore. They'd rather go thirsty.
Two of the elders say they've been dreaming the same dream. Won't say what it's about.
Someone carved symbols into the standing stone overnight. Fresh cuts, pale against the dark rock.
The bell in the watchtower rang on its own. Twice. No wind, no one near it.
Every compass in the trader's stock points the wrong way now. Has for about four days.
The shadows fall strange near the crossroads. Longer than they should be, even at noon.
A sinkhole opened behind the storehouse. Perfectly round, like something pushed up from underneath.
It started small. A bad feeling. Now half the settlement is sleeping with a blade under the pillow.
